rule: hit iff kw>=2 OR sem>=0.7 OR func>=0.7; total_hits >= 1 => FAIL

results.title: SFT-then-RL vs Mixed-Policy
results.rationale: Directly compares SFT-then-RL vs alternating-SFT-RL training. The candidate's phase-interleaved alternation between precision-SFT and speed-RL tracks is the same alternating-SFT-RL paradigm. High semantic and functional similarity; both alternate SFT and RL phases per epoch.

results.title: BRIDGE Cooperative SFT+RL Bilevel
results.rationale: Cooperative bilevel SFT+RL with SFT (precision/golden) and RL (policy optimization) as paired branches. Functionally equivalent to candidate's dual-track A (SFT) + B (RL) decomposition with periodic combination. The combination mechanism differs (bilevel vs task-arithmetic merge), but the two-track decomposition is the same.

results.title: Alternating SFT-RL Updates
results.rationale: Explicitly proposes alternating SFT+RL updates for faster convergence. Candidate's per-epoch alternation between SFT track and RL track is essentially this method. Very high overlap; the candidate's twist is task-arithmetic merge every 4 epochs, but the alternating scheme is identical.

results.title: Iterative RL/SFT Bridging
results.rationale: Iterative SFT-RL paradigm matches candidate's interleaved/iterative epoch-level alternation between SFT precision and RL speed phases. Functional overlap on the core scheduling pattern.

results.title: Streaming Merging ARM
results.rationale: Non-linear activation-guided rotation merging operator. Functionally overlaps with candidate's checkpoint merge step (combining two model branches into one). The merge operator differs (rotation vs linear task-arithmetic), but the role of periodic branch merging is shared.

results.title: SFT-RL Post-Training Quagmires
results.rationale: Analyzes SFT+RL post-training pipelines including alternating and combined schedules. Candidate's design falls within the analyzed space (alternating SFT and RL with periodic checkpointing). Semantic and functional scope overlap.

results.title: On-Policy Distillation
results.rationale: Combines RL with distillation (precision-like signal from teacher). Functionally overlaps with candidate's combination of precision-SFT (golden references) and on-policy RL signal. Both blend a precision/teacher-style supervised signal with on-policy reinforcement.

results.title: Multi-task LLM Data-Mix vs Model-Merge
results.rationale: Directly studies model-merging via task-arithmetic vs data-mixing for multi-task training. The candidate's merge step θ_merged = α·θ_A + (1-α)·θ_B is exactly the task-arithmetic linear combination operator analyzed here. Very high functional overlap on the merge mechanism.

reason: total_hits = 8 >= 1. Every web result triggers a hit via semantic >= 0.7 and/or functional >= 0.7. The candidate decomposes into well-known primitives: (a) alternating/interleaved SFT+RL training (results 1, 2, 3, 4, 6) and (b) task-arithmetic linear checkpoint merging (results 5, 8). The 'precision-track + speed-track' framing maps directly to SFT (golden refs) + RL (reward) decompositions already in the literature; periodic linear merge θ = α·θ_A + (1-α)·θ_B with validation-tuned α is the canonical task-arithmetic primitive. None of the tahitian/heiva/polynesian dance content_words appear in technical results (kw=0 across the board), but the rule fires on semantic/functional thresholds.
